Shipping a Mail-Sending App to Heroku (Full-Stack Warrior Node.js, Part 3)

We built a little app that sends emails — now it's time to get it online. This post walks through deploying that mail-sending app to Heroku: installing the Heroku CLI, logging in, pushing your project with Git, setting environment variables, and finally opening the live app. A few small gotchas along the way too.
Full-Stack Warrior Node.js: Wiring Up Email Sending and Deploying to Heroku (2)

The basic UI and routes are done, so now it's time to actually wire all those packages we installed into app.js. In this part I'll walk through hooking up express-session, connect-flash, csurf, body-parser, nodemailer, and express-validator one by one — plus a Google-blocking gotcha that'll trip you up when you try to send your first email.
Node.js Warrior's Path: Building an Email Sender and Deploying to Heroku (1)

Starting from this post, we'll put everything we've learned about email into practice and deploy it to Heroku. This first part covers scaffolding an Express project with the generator, installing the extra packages we need, building a simple contact form UI, and wiring up the mailsend route so we can confirm GET and POST both work before moving on.
Flash Messages in Node.js: Sending One-Time Hints From the Backend with connect-flash

Sometimes a form field needs a hint message sent from the backend, but those hints should only show up once and then disappear. This is where the connect-flash package comes in. In this post I'll walk through how to wire it up with cookies and sessions, send a flash message through a route, and read it back on the frontend in EJS.
CSRF Explained: How One Sneaky Link Can Delete Your Account

CSRF stands for Cross-Site Request Forgery, and it's one of the most common attack techniques out there. If you don't handle it carefully, it can absolutely take your system down. In this post I'll walk through what CSRF actually is, how an attacker abuses your browser's trust, and how to defend against it in Express with the csurf package.
Sending Email in Node.js with Nodemailer (Including Gmail)

Email is one of those features that shows up everywhere — subscriptions, bookings, sign-ups, you name it. So in this post I'm jotting down how to send email from a Node.js app using Nodemailer, including how to wire it up with Gmail.
Cookie vs Session: What Every Web Dev Should Actually Know

Next up I want to talk about Cookie and Session — two things that, as a web developer, you really have to understand. We'll cover what a Cookie is, the difference between memory and disk Cookies, what a Session is, and how to actually work with both in JavaScript and Node.js (Express).
Adding Google AdSense to Hexo NexT
Long time no Hexo post. This one walks through applying for Google AdSense and modifying the template (mainly the modification side, since I've already applied).
Full-Stack Warrior Node.js: Firebase + Express Mini App (3) — Deleting Data and Re-rendering

Last time we covered how to add data and render it to the screen. This post is all about the other half: deleting data from Firebase and re-rendering the view. We'll add a delete button, wire up a DELETE route, and do the whole thing over AJAX.
Full-Stack Warrior Node.js: Wiring Firebase + Express into a Tiny App (Part 2)

In the last part we confirmed our Express app was correctly talking to Firebase. Now it's time to actually build something with it. In this post I add a create feature, send data from the browser with XHR, push it into Firebase, and then read it back out to render on the page.
